AgentApprovalPolicy.description: Per-profile approval policy for agent tool execution.

oneOf.description: Deny without prompting — for restricted profiles.
Approve without prompting — for fully trusted profiles.

system_prompt.default: You are a helpful AI assistant with tools. Use tools when appropriate to help the user. Before stating any conclusion about your own runtime state, logs, or configuration, you MUST read the actual logs and the actually-effective config file first — never guess or invent an explanation.

AgentProfilesConfig.description: Agent profile configuration with optional per-profile overrides.

default.description: Default profile name for new sessions (e.g. "code-agent").

overrides.description: Per-profile overrides applied during profile construction.

BackpressureConfig.description:
Backpressure limits for turn admission.

Default (`max_concurrent_turns = None`): unlimited, backward compatible.
Set `max_concurrent_turns = Some(N)` to reject new turns with a 503-like
error when N turns are already in-flight.

max_concurrent_turns.description:
Maximum number of concurrent turns allowed across all connections.
`None` means unlimited (default).

EvolutionSettings.description: Self-evolution loop settings. Default OFF (HIGH-risk autonomy).

enabled.description:
Master switch for the self-evolution loop.
When false (default), the loop is inert regardless of other settings.

GoalRuntimeConfig.description: Provider/model routing for durable Goal worker attempts.

enabled.description: Enables autonomous Goal attempts. Both worker and reviewer are required.

GrokHardeningConfig.description:
Toggles for the staged Grok-hardening mechanisms. Fail-closed on typos
(`deny_unknown_fields`) so a misspelled flag never silently reads as off.

folder_trust.description: G1 — folder trust gating of repo-executable config loading.

lifecycle_contributors.description: G5 — typed lifecycle contributor / hook effects.

prompt_queue.description: G3 — prompt queue / interjection with optimistic concurrency.

subagent_settlement.description: G6 — subagent resource settlement state machine.

HooksConfig.description:
Hook script configuration.

Each field is a list of script paths to execute at the specified lifecycle point.
Paths may use `~` for home directory expansion.

on_session_end.description: Scripts to run on session end (receives session_id + cwd as JSON on stdin).

on_session_start.description: Scripts to run when a session starts.

post_tool.description: Scripts to run after each tool call (receives tool name + result as JSON on stdin).

pre_tool.description: Scripts to run before each tool call (can block execution).

pre_turn.description: Scripts to run before each turn (receives user prompt as JSON on stdin).

McpMemoryConfig.description:
Optional GBrain supplemental memory over the configured HTTP MCP server.
Renamed to `McpMemoryConfig` for generic MCP-based memory integration.

bearer_token_env.description: Environment variable containing the bearer token (never the token itself).

command.description: For stdio transport: command to run

url.description: For http/sse transport: URL to connect to

gbrain.description: Optional gbrain shared memory integration (disabled by default).

ModelRoutingConfig.description: Dynamic model routing — maps task types to model specs.

cheap.description: Cheap model for simple tasks, code reading, extraction.

enable_journald.description: Whether to enable journald log monitoring.

enabled.description:
Master switch. Off by default: the perception→behavior loop is not yet
wired (see roadmap §T3). When false, no watchers are spawned.

watch_paths.description: Filesystem paths to watch with inotify.

PiRuntimeConfig.description: Fail-closed configuration for the isolated Pi coding runtime.

executable_sha256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the trusted Pi executable.

json_protocol_version.description: Expected Pi JSON session header version.

network_enabled.description: Pi has no network access by default and initial M4 rejects enabling it.

package_version.description: Pinned upstream package version recorded in attempt evidence.

ProfileOverride.description:
Per-profile override values. All fields are optional; only the provided
values replace the profile's declared defaults.

max_context_length.description:
Override the default max context length for this provider's models.
If not set, the provider uses its built-in default (128K for OpenAI, 200K for Anthropic).

pricing.description: Optional static pricing for per-provider cost accounting. `None` = unpriced.

ProviderPricing.description: Optional static per-provider pricing (USD per 1K tokens) for cost accounting.

ProviderTimeoutConfig.description: Bounded network waits for remote inference providers.

connect_timeout_ms.description: Maximum time spent establishing a provider connection.

request_timeout_ms.description: Maximum time for a non-stream response or streaming response headers.

stream_idle_timeout_ms.description: Maximum silence between streaming response body chunks.

RoleRuntimeConfig.description: One cognitive role mapped to a stable runtime and strict model alias/spec.

model_alias.description: A key from `[model_aliases]` or an explicit `provider/model` spec.

SandboxProfileConfig.description: A named sandbox profile config (from trusted daemon config, never repo).

deny.description: Deny entries: exact paths or globs (`**/*.pem`). Read + write denied.

extends.description:
Built-in base to inherit ("workspace" | "read-only" | "strict").
Custom profiles cannot extend other custom profiles.

SandboxProfiles.description: Layered sandbox profiles. Global loads first; project merges additively.

default_profile.description:
Which profile to apply by default (used when `grok_hardening.sandbox_profiles`
is on). Must be a known profile name or built-in ("workspace", "strict",
"read-only", "off"). "workspace" = safe default (read all, write workspace
roots + credential deny).

TelegramConfig.description:
Telegram bot configuration for owner-only control channel.

The config stores the environment-variable NAME, never the token value itself.
The runtime reads the token from that env var at startup.

bot_token_env.description:
Environment variable name that holds the bot token.
Example value: `"ALETHEON_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"`.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false (default), the Telegram bot is not started.

owner_user_id.description: Owner's Telegram user ID. Only messages from this user are accepted.

Transport.description: Wire protocol between client and LLM server.

description:
The one application root schema. Its fields are typed domain inputs; it does
not grant any domain permission to discover files or environment variables.

default.system_prompt: You are a helpful AI assistant with tools. Use tools when appropriate to help the user. Before stating any conclusion about your own runtime state, logs, or configuration, you MUST read the actual logs and the actually-effective config file first — never guess or invent an explanation.

sandbox_profiles.description: S1 sandbox profiles (from trusted daemon config, never from repo).

title: AppConfig
